_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- determine the right balance between the organisation's cyber and information security capabilities, acceptable level of risk and speed of technology progress
- decide areas that need security investment
- create a security roadmap that enables the organisation to achieve its objectives
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- explaining the mutual benefits of the security strategy to a range of stakeholders and teams to bring them onboard
- advising other leaders on how to integrate security requirements in their strategies
- ensuring security is part of all the organisation's initiatives, despite competing priorities
- gaining support for investment in security from other leaders
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI systems, attack surface analysis, and control gap identification.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.475), RM-T-002 (RM, cosine: 0.462).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.4/10 (raw 0.536). Shared vocabulary: [security, skill]. DDaT-only terms [acceptable, achieve, advising, areas, balance, benefits] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, control] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.832 (P=0.817, R=0.848). R exceeds P by 0.031 — the WIDAI KSA's 19 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 106 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "You can:
- determine the right balance between the organisation's cyber and info" and "Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI sy" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.495 ≈ STS 0.536 (gap 0.041). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [security, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.9%. Shared tokens: [security, skill]. DDaT-only: [acceptable, achieve, advising, areas, balance, benefits]. WIDAI-only: [ai, analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, control].
